channel.title: Photo Feeds
channel.description: Combined feed of recent photo essays from The Atlantic, The Guardian, The Boston Globe, NYT, and Reuters.

item.title: [The Atlantic — Photography] Photos: A Bear-Emergency-Response Drill in Japan
item.description:
Kim Kyung-Hoon / Reuters — A man wearing a bear costume lies on the ground, playing dead after a simulated hunt during a bear-emergency-response drill in Yaita, Japan, on June 17, 2026.

Source: The Atlantic — Photography

item.title: [The Atlantic — Photography] Photos: A Bear-Emergency-Response Drill in Japan
item.description:
Kim Kyung-Hoon / Reuters — Hunters and a police officer practice their approach while poking the simulated bear.

Source: The Atlantic — Photography

item.title: [The Atlantic — Photography] Photos: A Bear-Emergency-Response Drill in Japan
item.description:
Kim Kyung-Hoon / Reuters — The simulated bear is down.

Source: The Atlantic — Photography

item.title: [The Atlantic — Photography] Photos: A Bear-Emergency-Response Drill in Japan
item.description:

Source: The Atlantic — Photography

item.description:

item.title: [The Atlantic — Photography] Photos: A Bear-Emergency-Response Drill in Japan
item.description:
Kim Kyung-Hoon / Reuters — A man in a bear costume takes part in an emergency-response drill simulating a bear intrusion in Yaita, Tochigi Prefecture, Japan, June 17, 2026.

Source: The Atlantic — Photography

Obbürgen, Switzerland — Pakistan’s prime minister, Shehbaz Sharif, shakes hands with Donald Trump’s son-in-law, Jared Kushner, in front of the US vice-president, JD Vance, and special envoy Steve Witkoff before the start of talks with Iranian officialsShare. — Photograph: Nathan Howard/Reuters

Los Angeles, US — Smoke rises from a fire at a cold storage facility in the Boyle Heights neighbourhood of the city Share. — Photograph: Ted Soqui/EPA

item.title: [The Guardian — Photos of the Day] The week around the world in 20 pictures
item.source: The Guardian — Photos of the Day
item.description:
Versailles, France — Donald Trump receives a tour of Chateau de Versailles from Emmanuel Macron ahead of a dinner following the conclusion of the G7 summit. Trump responded to criticism of his ceasefire deal with Iran, warning at the summit that he was prepared to go back to dropping bombs and insisting the deal did not require the US to pay even 10 cents to IranShare. — Photograph: Anna Moneymaker/Reuters

Source: The Guardian — Photos of the Day

item.title: [The Guardian — Photos of the Day] The week around the world in 20 pictures
item.source: The Guardian — Photos of the Day
item.description:
Chicago, US — Sasha Obama, Malia Obama, Michelle Obama, Barack Obama, Bill Clinton and Hillary Clinton attend the dedication ceremony for the opening of the Barack Obama Presidential Center in John Lewis Plaza in Chicago. Featuring appearances by a cast of musical stars and retired politicians from a less polarised era, it was a seemingly perfect antidote to the spectacle of cage fights on the White House lawnShare. — Photograph: Win McNamee/Getty Images

Source: The Guardian — Photos of the Day

item.title: [The Guardian — Photos of the Day] The week around the world in 20 pictures
item.source: The Guardian — Photos of the Day
item.description:
Washington, DC, US — Diego Lopes celebrates after defeating Steve Garcia in a featherweight bout during the UFC Freedom 250 mixed martial arts event on the South Lawn of the White House. Beneath a giant steel canopy known as the Claw, with military flyovers overhead and thousands of spectators spread across the South Lawn and nearby Ellipse, Donald Trump celebrated his 80th birthday and the forthcoming semiquincentennial by staging the first professional sporting event in White House historyShare. — Photograph: Chip Somodevilla/Getty Images

Source: The Guardian — Photos of the Day

item.title: [The Guardian — Photos of the Day] The week around the world in 20 pictures
item.source: The Guardian — Photos of the Day
item.description:
Kansas City, US — Argentina’s Lionel Messi enters the pitch before the 2026 World Cup match against Algeria at the Kansas City Stadium. Messi scored a hat-trick to draw level with Germany’s Miroslav Klose for the World Cup’s all-time mark of 16 goalsShare. — Photograph: Roberto Schmidt/AFP/Getty Images

Source: The Guardian — Photos of the Day

item.title: [The Guardian — Photos of the Day] The week around the world in 20 pictures
item.source: The Guardian — Photos of the Day
item.description:
Dallas, US — England’s Jude Bellingham celebrates with Harry Kane and Noni Madueke after scoring against Croatia in their World Cup match at Dallas Stadium. Bellingham was in the mood throughout the game, determined to play without fear and to the point of maximum expression, and it was the midfielder that tilted the contest in England’s favour with a storming run and finishShare. — Photograph: MI News/NurPhoto/Shutterstock

Source: The Guardian — Photos of the Day

item.title: [The Guardian — Photos of the Day] The week around the world in 20 pictures
item.source: The Guardian — Photos of the Day
item.description:
New York City, US — New York Knicks fans celebrate the team’s NBA championship during their first-ever paradeShare.

Source: The Guardian — Photos of the Day

item.title: [The Guardian — Photos of the Day] The week around the world in 20 pictures
item.source: The Guardian — Photos of the Day
item.description:
Boborás, Spain — An active wildfire burns in the north-western Spanish region of Galicia as firefighters ctry to contain a blazeShare. — Photograph: Brais Lorenzo/Zuma/Shutterstock

Source: The Guardian — Photos of the Day

item.title: [The Guardian — Photos of the Day] The week around the world in 20 pictures
item.source: The Guardian — Photos of the Day
item.description:
Bitlis, Turkey — Sheep herds are driven toward the summer pastures of Nemrut mountain from the village of KiyiduzuShare. — Photograph: Mustafa Kilic/Anadolu/Getty Images

Source: The Guardian — Photos of the Day

item.title: [The Guardian — Photos of the Day] The week around the world in 20 pictures
item.source: The Guardian — Photos of the Day
item.description:
Mongbwalu, Democratic Republic of the Congo — Female miners, known locally as decalots, wear plastic bags over their clothing to protect themselves from mud and dirt as they sort out rocks containing gold particles at an artisanal mining site. A month on since the latest outbreak of Ebola was first identified in the DRC, the latest figures from the UN show 676 confirmed cases and 136 deathsShare. — Photograph: Jospin Mwisha/AFP/Getty Images

Source: The Guardian — Photos of the Day

item.title: [The Guardian — Photos of the Day] The week around the world in 20 pictures
item.source: The Guardian — Photos of the Day
item.description:
London, UK — A supporter of the banned protest group Palestine Action is arrested by police during a demonstration outside the high court in London. The home secretary’s decision to ban Palestine Action was lawful, the court of appeal has ruled. A five-strong panel, including the two most senior judges in England and Wales, overturned February’s decision of the high court that the proscription of the direct action group, the first to be banned under the Terrorism Act, was wrongShare. — Photograph: Neil Hall/EPA

Source: The Guardian — Photos of the Day

item.title: [The Guardian — Photos of the Day] The week around the world in 20 pictures
item.source: The Guardian — Photos of the Day
item.description:
Geneva, Switzerland — A demonstrator throws branches in the fire of a burning trash container during a rally of a No-G7 coalition of more than 60 associations, unions and leftwing groups ‘against fascism and imperialism’, before the start of the G7 summit in Évian-les-BainsShare. — Photograph: Gabriel Monnet/AFP/Getty Images

Source: The Guardian — Photos of the Day

item.title: [The Guardian — Photos of the Day] The week around the world in 20 pictures
item.source: The Guardian — Photos of the Day
item.description:
Kfar Yona, Israel — Israeli security forces stand guard as ultra-Orthodox Jews gather to protest against military conscription outside Beit Lid military prisonShare. — Photograph: Ilia Yefimovich/AFP/Getty Images

Source: The Guardian — Photos of the Day

item.title: [The Guardian — Photos of the Day] The week around the world in 20 pictures
item.source: The Guardian — Photos of the Day
item.description:
Gaza — Zeina al-Habil hugs the body of her father after he and her five-year-old brother were killed in an Israeli missile strike that targeted their home in the neighbourhood of Sheikh Radwan. The International Committee of the Red Cross said this week the risk that the thousands of Palestinians buried beneath Gaza’s rubble may never be identified is increasing by the day, as recovery efforts remain slow and many victims have yet to be retrieved.Share. — Photograph: Omar al-Qattaa/AFP/Getty Images

Source: The Guardian — Photos of the Day

item.title: [The Guardian — Photos of the Day] The week around the world in 20 pictures
item.source: The Guardian — Photos of the Day
item.description:
Tyre, Lebanon — A woman walks past rubble at the site of an Israeli strike. Talks due to take place on Friday between the US ⁠and ​Iran in Switzerland to implement a peace deal were cancelled as Hezbollah killed four Israeli soldiers and Israel carried out a wave of retaliatory airstrikes in south Lebanon and the Bekaa valley that killed at least 18 peopleShare. — Photograph: Zohra Bensemra/Reuters

Source: The Guardian — Photos of the Day

item.title: [The Guardian — Photos of the Day] The week around the world in 20 pictures
item.source: The Guardian — Photos of the Day
item.description:
Moscow, Russia — A woman walks outside a shopping mall as black smoke rises from Gazprom Neft’s oil refinery in the south-eastern outskirts of Moscow. Ukrainian drones hit several locations across the city in Kyiv’s biggest air raid on the capital since the start of Russia’s full-scale invasion, setting a major ⁠oil refinery on fire and forcing evacuations at the country’s largest airportShare. — Photograph: AFP/Getty Images

Source: The Guardian — Photos of the Day

item.title: [The Guardian — Photos of the Day] The week around the world in 20 pictures
item.source: The Guardian — Photos of the Day
item.description:
Kyiv, Ukraine — A woman reacts as she looks at residential houses damaged after a Russian strike on Kyiv. Five people were killed on Sunday night in the city when waves of drones and missiles drove residents to underground shelters and heavy explosions echoed throughout the capitalShare. — Photograph: Evgeniy Maloletka/AP

Source: The Guardian — Photos of the Day

item.title: [The Guardian — Photos of the Day] The week around the world in 20 pictures
item.source: The Guardian — Photos of the Day
item.description:
Kyiv, Ukraine — Firefighters attempt to put out a fire at the Dormition Cathedral in the Pechersk Lavra monastery complex, a Unesco world heritage site and one of Ukraine’s most significant religious and cultural sites, following a Russian strikeShare. — Photograph: Evgeniy Maloletka/AP

Source: The Guardian — Photos of the Day

item.title: [The Guardian — Photos of the Day] The week around the world in 20 pictures
item.source: The Guardian — Photos of the Day
item.description:
Kyiv, Ukraine — Firefighters try to put out a fire at a city marketplace following a Russian missile and drone attack in Kyiv. Before the attack, amid evidence of mounting battlefield setbacks for Russia, President Vladimir Putin had warned that Moscow would target Ukraine with ‘systemic’ strikesShare. — Photograph: Dan Bashakov/AP

Source: The Guardian — Photos of the Day

Soccer Football - FIFA World Cup 2026 - Group A - Mexico v South Africa - Estadio Azteca, Mexico City, Mexico - June 11, 2026 General view during the opening ceremony before the match REUTERS/Kai Pfaffenbach

item.title: [The Atlantic — Photography] Photos of the Week: Chair Hat, Shouting Choir, Demolition Derby
item.description:
Tony Karumba / AFP / Getty — A woman forages for shells along the beach at low tide, under an imposing hanging sea cliff, looking for materials to make traditional jewelry to sell to visiting tourists on Wasini-Mkwiro Island, off Kenya’s coast, on June 14, 2026.

Source: The Atlantic — Photography

item.title: [The Atlantic — Photography] Photos of the Week: Chair Hat, Shouting Choir, Demolition Derby
item.description:
Mustafa Kilic / Anadolu / Getty — Sheep are driven toward the summer pastures of Mount Nemrut from Kiyiduzu village, in the Tatvan district of Bitlis, Turkey, on June 12, 2026.

Source: The Atlantic — Photography

item.title: [The Atlantic — Photography] Photos of the Week: Chair Hat, Shouting Choir, Demolition Derby
item.description:
Gary Hershorn / Getty — A lightning bolt strikes One World Trade Center during a thunderstorm in New York City on June 14, 2026, as seen from Jersey City, New Jersey.

Source: The Atlantic — Photography

item.title: [The Atlantic — Photography] Photos of the Week: Chair Hat, Shouting Choir, Demolition Derby
item.description:
Marco Ugarte / AP — A duck named Merlin, dressed in a Mexican-national-soccer-team jersey, follows his caretaker Christian Gomez as they cross the street in Mexico City, on June 15, 2026.

Source: The Atlantic — Photography

item.title: [The Atlantic — Photography] Photos of the Week: Chair Hat, Shouting Choir, Demolition Derby
item.description:
Hendrik Schmidt / DPA / Getty — Members of the Finnish men’s choir Mieskuoro Huutajat perform their repertoire by shouting at the opening of the “Theater der Welt” festival on Theaterplatz, in Chemnitz, Germany, on June 18, 2026. The choir does not sing in the traditional sense, but rather shouts, roars, or calls out.

Source: The Atlantic — Photography

item.title: [The Atlantic — Photography] Photos of the Week: Chair Hat, Shouting Choir, Demolition Derby
item.description:
Joel Saget / AFP / Getty — A visitor looks at the latest artwork of the French artist JR, La Caverne du Pont Neuf (“The Pont Neuf Cave”) on the Pont Neuf bridge, in Paris on June 15, 2026.

Source: The Atlantic — Photography

item.title: [The Atlantic — Photography] Photos of the Week: Chair Hat, Shouting Choir, Demolition Derby
item.description:
Rasid Necati Aslim / Anadolu / Getty — Visitors show off their elaborate hats and elegant outfits during the third day of the traditional Royal Ascot horse-racing event at Ascot Racecourse, in Berkshire, England, on June 18, 2026.

Source: The Atlantic — Photography

item.title: [The Atlantic — Photography] Photos of the Week: Chair Hat, Shouting Choir, Demolition Derby
item.description:
Scott Olson / Getty — Corn grows in a field near Weissert, Nebraska, on June 14, 2026. According to the U.S. Drought Monitor, more than 80 percent of Nebraska is experiencing moderate-to-exceptional drought conditions, severely affecting farmers and ranchers across the state.

Source: The Atlantic — Photography

item.title: [The Atlantic — Photography] Photos of the Week: Chair Hat, Shouting Choir, Demolition Derby
item.description:
Tercio Teixeira / AFP / Getty — An aerial view shows the site of a helicopter crash in the Recreio dos Bandeirantes neighborhood, in Rio de Janeiro, Brazil, on June 14, 2026. At least six people died after two helicopters crashed after a suspected midair collision, firefighters said.

Source: The Atlantic — Photography

item.title: [The Atlantic — Photography] Photos of the Week: Chair Hat, Shouting Choir, Demolition Derby
item.description:
Gregory Bull / AP — Switzerland’s Dan Ndoye tries to warm up as sprinklers water the field ahead the World Cup Group B soccer match between Switzerland and Bosnia, in Inglewood, California, near Los Angeles, on June 18, 2026.

Source: The Atlantic — Photography

item.title: [The Atlantic — Photography] Photos of the Week: Chair Hat, Shouting Choir, Demolition Derby
item.description:
Punit Paranjpe / AFP / Getty — An aerial view of fishing boats anchored near Uttan village, on the outskirts of Mumbai, on June 14, 2026, following an annual 61-day fishing ban imposed along India’s coastal region to protect marine life during their peak breeding period in the monsoons

Source: The Atlantic — Photography

item.title: [The Atlantic — Photography] Photos of the Week: Chair Hat, Shouting Choir, Demolition Derby
item.description:
Zhang Xiangyi / China News Service / VCG / Getty — Dragon-boat teams compete during the Eastern District Dragon Boat Race in Hong Kong, China, on June 14, 2026.

item.description:
Scott Olson / Getty — Travis Bostock heads to the pits after competing in the final heat of the Wreckless Promotions demolition derby at the Custer County Fairgrounds on June 13, 2026, in Broken Bow, Nebraska.

item.description:
Darko Bandic / AP — Locals watch a World Cup soccer match between England and Croatia projected on a barn wall, in Kumrovec, Northern Croatia, on June 17, 2026.

item.title: [The Atlantic — Photography] Photos of the Week: Chair Hat, Shouting Choir, Demolition Derby
item.description:
Timur Matahari / AFP / Getty — Young people take part in a traditional “fire football” game using a burning coconut shell during celebrations marking the beginning of the Islamic New Year in Bandung, West Java, on June 15, 2026.

item.description:
John Locher / AP — The Carolina Hurricanes pose for photos after a win over the Vegas Golden Knights in Game 6 of the NHL Stanley Cup Final series, on June 14, 2026, in Las Vegas.

item.description:
Cheng Chia Huang / Getty — A fire show takes place at the Dragon Boat Festival in Tainan, Taiwan, on June 17, 2026.

item.description:
Sefa Karacan / Anadolu / Getty — Black smoke rises from a refinery where a fire broke out following Ukrainian missile and drone strikes, as firefighting efforts continue in Moscow, Russia, on June 18, 2026.

item.title: [The Atlantic — Photography] Photos of the Week: Chair Hat, Shouting Choir, Demolition Derby
item.description:
Evgeniy Maloletka / AP — Firefighters work to put out a fire at the Dormition Cathedral of the Kyiv Pechersk Lavra after it was hit during Russian missile and drone strikes, in Kyiv, Ukraine, on June 15, 2026.

item.title: [The Atlantic — Photography] Photos of the Week: Chair Hat, Shouting Choir, Demolition Derby
item.description:
Vera Danchenko / Anadolu / Getty — The frigate Rossiya, with scarlet sails, floats on the Neva River during a rehearsal for the Scarlet Sails celebration, in St. Petersburg, Russia, on June 11, 2026.

Source: The Atlantic — Photography

item.title: [The Atlantic — Photography] Photos of the Week: Chair Hat, Shouting Choir, Demolition Derby
item.description:
Harold Cunningham / Getty — A visitor walks past the Ryan Gander artwork I’ve Felt Everything I’m Going to Feel—The Unspeakable World during a press preview of the 2026 Art Basel, in Basel, Switzerland, on June 16, 2026.

Source: The Atlantic — Photography

item.title: [The Atlantic — Photography] Photos of the Week: Chair Hat, Shouting Choir, Demolition Derby
item.description:
Cliff Owen / AP — A worker removes a letter from President Trump’s name from the wall of the John F. Kennedy Center for the Performing Arts, in Washington, D.C., on June 13, 2026.

Source: The Atlantic — Photography

item.title: [The Atlantic — Photography] Photos of the Week: Chair Hat, Shouting Choir, Demolition Derby
item.description:
Chip Somodevilla / Getty — National Park Service employees and contractors use vacuums to remove green algae from the bottom of the Lincoln Memorial Reflecting Pool on June 18, 2026, in Washington, D.C. The NPS is working to control and remove the algae bloom that has turned the pool green following the Trump administration’s recent $14 million repair, resealing, and painting project.

Source: The Atlantic — Photography

item.title: [The Atlantic — Photography] Photos of the Week: Chair Hat, Shouting Choir, Demolition Derby
item.description:
